publication.title: Kungfu: Verified Continuity for Agent Work
publication.abstract: Project Cuts, an embeddable runtime, and an open responsibility standard for Agent work that outlives the chat.

sectionMap.title: Executive Summary: The Work Must Outlive the Chat
subsections: One Product Decision, Two Adoption Paths
sectionMap.summary: Agents can already produce valuable code, research, plans, operations, and decisions. Yet the dominant product container is still a chat session. When that session closes, the next Agent often receives a transcript, a summary, or an unverified memory instead of an exact statement of what the project has become. The model may be capable while the work remains fragile.

sectionMap.title: The Continuity Gap
sectionMap.summary: Real work is not a prompt-response pair. It has accepted state, causal history, direction, authority, evidence, unresolved conflict, and consequences. It may cross context windows, models, devices, repositories, organizations, and years. A session is only one observer's temporary projection of that work.

sectionMap.title: The Strategic Choice for Agent Builders
subsections: What Embedding Does and Does Not Mean
sectionMap.summary: An Agent vendor already owns the scarce product assets: the interface, model experience, accounts, permissions, policies, cloud, distribution, and customer relationship. It should preserve those assets. The strategic question is not whether to become a Kungfu-branded application. It is whether to spend years reconstructing a durable runtime before its users can keep work across chats.

sectionMap.title: Project Cut: The First User Object
subsections: A Settlement, Not a Fourth Fact Engine
sectionMap.summary: The first user layer in Kungfu is not a log viewer, model selector, or internal Fact table. It is the Project Cut: the highest ordinary interface through which a person or Agent inspects what a project has formally become and from where it may continue. This product decision keeps rigor below the surface while giving the user one actionable coordinate above it.

sectionMap.title: How Work Continues Across Sessions
sectionMap.summary: Continuity is a closed loop, not a saved transcript. Kungfu's target product loop is:

sectionMap.title: The Runtime Beneath the Cut
subsections: Buildchain Is Release Evidence, Not Runtime State
sectionMap.summary: Project Cut is the first user object, but it can be trusted only because its inputs and lifecycle remain verifiable beneath the product surface. libkungfu is the reference implementation of that runtime substrate. It owns how local Facts and Episodes are recorded, ordered, stored, queried, verified, exported, and recovered while the embedding application owns its domain facts and product decisions.

sectionMap.title: KFD and Independent Agent Hubs
sectionMap.summary: KFD---Kung Fu Decisions---is an open engineering standard for a small, testable foundation for reliable action and continuity under uncertainty. It is a canonical public decision registry, not a Kungfu product API, a central service, a universal workflow language, or a belief test. Products, organizations, humans, Agents, and other bounded systems can adopt exact decisions within a declared scope and publish their own mappings, evidence, qualification, and residual risk.

sectionMap.title: Authority, Trust, and Evidence
sectionMap.summary: Continuity is unsafe when a system collapses transport, identity, occurrence, judgment, and authority into a single success flag. Kungfu and KFD preserve the separations that keep responsibility attributable.

sectionMap.title: Current State, Adoption, and Roadmap
subsections: The Builder Path: Inspect, Embed, Prove, Connect
sectionMap.summary: This paper is an alpha positioning and architecture document. It recommends immediate implementation work while keeping capability claims below the public evidence ceiling.

sectionMap.title: Conclusion
sectionMap.summary: The market is moving from Agents that answer inside a chat to Agents that carry responsibility across real work. The decisive product question is no longer only how intelligent one session can be. It is whether the work remains verifiable, recoverable, and continuable after that session disappears.

verification.residualRisk: This is an alpha product and architecture paper based on first-party evidence; it does not establish external vendor adoption, certification, stable Hub interoperability, or broad production readiness.
